theme: what stays constant when the collision rigs differ
title: Collision-Rig Consortium — recovering the shared conserved quantity
strategies: trivial: predict post = pre (nothing happens) -> reproduces the checker baseline ~0.1

greedy: pooled black-box ridge regression of each post velocity on polynomial features (momenta m1*v1, m2*v2, damping interactions); interpolates inside the training mass range [1,8] but the true mass scaling m^alpha with alpha != 1 and the exp damping law extrapolate catastrophically to held-out masses 10-30, worst on hard ids where alpha is far from 1 and damping leaves the training band

strong: search INVARIANT space before predicting -- grid-search the shared exponent alpha that makes m1^a*v1 + m2^a*v2 constant across every rig's collisions; then per-rig restitution from the relative velocity, and a weighted log-linear fit ln e = ln e0 - beta*gamma across the 5 rigs (multi-experiment consistency); emit the closed-form two-body map with m^alpha and e0*exp(-beta*g), which survives the new mass regime

headroom above strong: the true restitution also decays with impact speed exp(-eta*|v2-v1|); a solver that models the |w| dependence (or jointly refines all four constants on train prediction error) pushes held-out MSE toward the sensor-noise floor
